content_of_post_to_comment: Can we please just let abuse survivors express their anger in safety? Can we please just rally around each other and tell each other it is safe to feel it? Can we please just get curious about womens anger? I get it, anger sucks, but it is a normal human emotion. It can lead us to better futures, to better boundaries, to realising what we will never let happen to us ever again. So why not let the transformative power of healthily expressed anger exist?
comment: Permission to express anger safely often presumes a container already exists. The harder question is who builds and maintains those boundaries so expression doesn’t drift into escalation or punishment.
